question.text: An investor constructs a bear put spread: buys 500 PE at Rs. 35 and sells 460 PE at Rs. 15. What is the maximum profit and maximum loss?

answer.text: Max profit = Rs. 20, Max loss = Rs. 20
Max profit = Rs. 40, Max loss = Rs. 35
Max profit = Rs. 35, Max loss = Rs. 15
Max profit = Rs. 20, Max loss = Rs. 40

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Step-by-step calculation: 1. Net premium paid = 35 - 15 = Rs. 20 (debit spread) 2. Maximum loss = Net premium paid = Rs. 20 (when stock stays above Rs. 500) 3. Maximum profit = Difference in strikes - Net premium = (500 - 460) - 20 = 40 - 20 = Rs. 20 (when stock falls below Rs. 460)
**The Trap:** Confusing the higher and lower strikes in put spread. In bear put spread, you buy the HIGHER strike put and sell the LOWER strike put (opposite of bull call).
**Cross-Exam Context:** Bear put and bull call spreads are mirror strategies - understanding one helps with the other.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Bear put spread: Buy higher strike put + Sell lower strike put. Max profit = Strike diff - Net premium. Max loss = Net premium.
